title: Will AI Ever Be GENERATIVE?
summary: A brief discussion about Ada Lovelace's objection to AI creativity, which Alan Turing addressed in his famous essay. The conversation explores whether AI systems like Lambda and GPT-3 can be truly generative or are merely reactive, comparing adaptive AI learning to genuine creative impulse.

key_claims.claim: Turing addresses Ada Lovelace's objection as one of nine objections in his essay
Lambda can go out and read up on topics it doesn't know about during a conversation and incorporate that learning into its language model
A main qualitative difference between Lambda and GPT-3 is Lambda's ability to actively learn during conversations
